title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno

units.text: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno — Which teacher is better for Intro to CS? Which teacher is easier to have a better grade in?

metadata.thread_title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno
metadata.author: OP

units.text: Intro CS is a standardized course. Projects and everything are the same (Blanking on if exams are too however). You can go to whichever professor lecture you want and be fine, I had Centeno and found hers to be more preferable (Goel wasn't a professor when I took the course so not sure)

metadata.thread_title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno
metadata.author: ScarletGingerrr

units.text: Projects, exams, recitations, etc are all coordinated for both CS111 and CS112

metadata.thread_title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno
metadata.author: XxBoatLickerxX

units.text: Like everyone’s said, CS111 is coordinated across all professors, so which one you pick is really just up to who’s lecture style you like (and you can attend any professor’s lecture you want - since again it’s all coordinated). I picked Goel for CS111 and 112 because her class fit into my schedule better, and I never attended anyone else’s lectures; Goel was really good at explaining things and very helpful answering questions after class. She was super kind and a great lecturer. With that being said, i’ve heard great things about Centeno as well and you can study with whoever.

metadata.thread_title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno
metadata.author: XxBoatLickerxX

units.text: I second this (as someone who took Goel for cs111 and content for cs112). Centeno I believe teaches at a slightly slower pace, but both teachers explain things well and give examples

metadata.thread_title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno
metadata.author: Story_Salamander

units.text: I’ve attended lectures from both but found Goel’s to be much more structured and easy to follow. Both professors are extremely helpful and kind though! Centeno is involved with some Women in CS stuff (not sure about Goel), so if that interests you then you may be able to form a good connection with her. Although I much preferred Goel’s lecturing, they are both wonderful and you can’t really go wrong.

metadata.thread_title: Intro to CS Goel or Centeno

units.text: i signed up for goel and also went to some centeno lectures when i could. i preferred goel cause i liked the way she worked out examples and explained stuff

metadata.author: phanticx

units.text: Goel 100%. She’s acc such a good prof. Centeno isn’t bad at all, very solid but she goes very slow and I often zoned out during her lectures. Also sometimes goes off topic too. Centeno is the leader of the course, but take Goel. I had her for Data Structures too which is notoriously a hard course at Rutgers and she made it a breeze for me

title: Preparation for Data Structures (CS 112) and Statistics 1

units.text: Preparation for Data Structures (CS 112) and Statistics 1 — Hello, I hope you are all having a great Winter Break! I was wondering if you all could help give me resources to prepare for Data Structures and Statistics 1, especially Data Structures. Also, for CS 112, I have seen that people suggest going over arrays, recursion, Big O runtimes, and the searching and sorting patterns as well. Do you all have suggestions as to which videos/websites I should visit. Would Professor Ana Paula Centeno's videos for CS 111 suffice? Thank you very much!

metadata.thread_title: Preparation for Data Structures (CS 112) and Statistics 1
metadata.author: OP

units.text: the data structures course page is public and has all the units listed in order along with lecture slides: https://ds.cs.rutgers.edu/

metadata.thread_title: Preparation for Data Structures (CS 112) and Statistics 1
metadata.author: trynumber53

units.text: I dunno about statistics, but I took data structures with Centeno last semester so I could give you some tips. Big O efficiencies are definitely covered in data structures, and you will be taught and expected to memorize the runtimes for the different data structures. Reviewing centeno's videos will help with this as well as for sorting algorithms (sorting algorithms aren't covered until the end of the semester though). Someone previously commented on visiting the website for data structures which has all the lecture slides. I'll repost the link here in case it gets lost among all the comments: https://ds.cs.rutgers.edu/ On the website there are exams from previous semesters as well as the answer keys. I dunno who you have for data structures this semester, but something centeno did for my class was draw out each data structure visually and it helped out a lot (her slides helped as well)! There are also a lot of useful videos online about data structures you could look up as well. I recommend Michael Sambol's videos: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL9xmBV_5YoZO2D89q42-y8voxIJKpB4oR&si=4NVCWWAnA5m_7j_h Neso Academy also has a few videos on data structures as well (I'm not sure if they will help cause I've only ever used their videos for discrete I). I hope all this helps! :)

metadata.thread_title: Preparation for Data Structures (CS 112) and Statistics 1
metadata.author: Story_Salamander

units.text: Haven't taken it here, but I'm pretty sure Statistics 1 is roughly equivalent to AP Statistics, for which there is a Khan Academy course.

title: Does it matter what CS112 professor i take in the spring in terms of workload

units.text: Data structures doesn’t have any group projects, you’re probably reading reviews from a different class that the prof teaches. But either way your professor for 112 doesn’t really matter because all of the exams and assignments are course coordinated for every section.

metadata.thread_title: Does it matter what CS112 professor i take in the spring in terms of workload
metadata.author: Terrible_Trouble_113

units.text: skip lecture and pick the cs112 section that has the best recitation time for you IMO

metadata.thread_title: Does it matter what CS112 professor i take in the spring in terms of workload
metadata.author: rhinopithecusBieti

units.text: This, I had all 8:30 am sections for lectures and recitation and it helped free up my days to study

metadata.thread_title: Does it matter what CS112 professor i take in the spring in terms of workload
metadata.author: Tight-Proposal-4787

units.text: I never remember getting a group project in cs112, make sure you check what class they are talking about and what year that lost the was on. Otherwise, no their workload was the same for me last year.

metadata.thread_title: Does it matter what CS112 professor i take in the spring in terms of workload
metadata.author: SandyShocker

units.text: The professor you choose doesn’t really matter in my opinion because they all teach the same content regardless but I had Lily Chang and she was good but I just couldn’t focus at 8:30 am to absorb everything taught. What is RMP because there were no group projects for CS112?

title: Mousumi Chakrabarty

units.text: great professor, just go to the lectures and you'll be fine :)
Cutest professor in the department, much recommend :3
Very easy class. Just pull up to all classes for the notes. Ask questions if needed but otherwise such as easy class.

You will 100% pass the class, however, you will have a difficult time understanding her and the curriculum. Grading is extremely lenient, however, her teaching skills are weak, and grasping the actual curriculum is challenging. Would not recommend if you need to truly learn the material, would recommend if you simply want the grade.

Taking Professor Chakrabarty was a great experience for Mobile App Development. She gave a lot of projects during class to showcase & write the code down. She made everything crystal clear, and the exams/projects were relatively easy. She is a really kind and caring professor. Do not hesitate to ask her any questions for any clarification.
She was really nice and helped me a lot when I had her for precalc.
She cute. 10/10 Got me feeling *o* ohhh

Genuinely one of the greatest teachers I have had at Rutgers-Newark. Ms. Chakrabarty is very caring and respectful and makes sure we all understand the material. I have nothing bad to say, this is my 2nd or 3rd class with her and she is very knowledgeable about her classes and topics. Her homework is very easy if you pay attention.

Professor Chakrabarty is a fantastic professor who makes sure all of her students understand the material, and she genuinely shows care in her teaching and wants her students to succeed. She is very accessible and makes it easy for students to ask for help.
One of the nicest professors you'll ever have. She never makes you feel stupid when it comes to help or questions.

Literally the best professor out there. I took her for cs 101 and now I have her for literally any class that is required. She's not just about making sure kids get good grades but she makes sure you understand the material as well and she goes out of her way to do that. If you get a chance to take her class, do it

One of the nicest, most caring professors I've ever met. She does her absolute best to make students understand the concepts more and give good examples in the lectures. I would definitely take her for any class she teaches.

Extremely helpful, material is straightforward and exams are derived from review. She is one of the nicest professors I've had. She will even help you with other courses if you come to her during her office hours. Wish she was teaching all of my classes. Definitely would take again!

She is amazing, She is the type of professor who makes sure the whole class understands, her quizzes/test are fair. As long as you study and go to class you will pass. Although she says you don't really need it, buy the book. Just remember the code she teaches and you should be fine, and if u get the book you will have coding examples for test.

title: Charles Edeki

units.text: Putting aside that he says some truly WEIRD things about women in class, nothing he lectures about shows up on the exams. It is pointless to go to lecture. Test questions are generated from the textbook and he does not check to make sure questions are fair &on the correct chapter. Chp 8 stuff will be on a chp 12 test, mind you he'll skip chap 4-9.

Awful professor. Only reads off slides, will get off topic often, and for no reason go on several-minute tangents about personal stuff nobody should know about him. He will hijack an hour-plus-long class and spend twenty minutes telling a story unprompted. This is an entirely self-taught class.

title: Joseph Elliot

units.text: I took Professor Elliot for Software Engineering. It was hard to hear him while teaching. The concepts he taught us were good, but the delivery was unfortunately not on point. Projects were relatively easy as there were plenty of resources, but they need to be updated. There's an in-class project/program you need to follow, but the class is easy.

Here's a clean, under-350-character version: Honestly sad how this guy is Director of CS. Absolute joke of a professor. He takes five classes to cover a 20-minute project, reads word-for-word from a free Apple textbook, and gets annoyed if you finish early. Perfect for the CS students who are middle eastern and don't care—you'll love him.

Absolute joke of a professor. Does not teach anything useful and does not care about his students at all. Will embarrass you in front of the class if you make a mistake or come in late. Will make you waste time to come to office hours for any concerns he deems, just to do nothing to help you and even add insult to injury. Deserves to be fired.

Only reads off the slides, provides nothing else. Regularly denigrates students for missing days, not doing bonus work, in front of other students. When presenting, regularly interrupts students and attempts to correct, even if he's wrong. A poor reflection of Rutgers as a whole, and would happily see him fired.
I love this guy, such a good professor.

If you don't want to learn anything he's your guy. He doesn't know jack shit. Plus he's somehow the head of the CS department still blows my mind. Save ur time and don't goto his office hrs. If you actually do want learn please do yourselves a favor and avoid him at all cost.

get ready to learn on your own, he doesn't know much more than you. get on his good side, and be nice. if you get whatever he asks you done, he will give you a grade. Our midterm was hard and most people did bad so he gave multiple chances to gain extra credit and also easier assignments to boost your grade. other reviews are mostly accurate!

i rated him before. please do not take him, he gave me a very low score on the midterm when our answers were legit the same and would not give me some points back when i politely asked if he can check again. TOUGH grader. he does not work with you. please save your money and time, take someone else!
Personally he's not the best nor is he the worst, but he is quite nice whenever it comes to questions. If you are someone who is independent and a self study person, then take him.

professor elliot is okay, he does have an accent but it is easy to understand. His lectures consist of him reading from the power-point slides. attendance is mandatory so try to arrive to class. his labs are 3% which is an easy 100 even if don't get it right. however, he does not know how to help students when it comes to the midterm or final exam.

This professor does not teach at all. He gave everyone the source code for the projects in the book and just told us to make it work for the projects. No help AT ALL when we ran into bugs, just tells you to try again. The grades are just based on the book projects, if you can't get them to run, good luck passing. Strongly suggest avoiding this guy.
Voice is monotone. Project based. Will take again.

[Joseph Elliot · 251COMP · Q1.0/D3.0 · Newark]
i rated him before. please do not take him, he gave me a very low score on the midterm when our answers were legit the same and would not give me some points back when i politely asked if he can check again. TOUGH grader. he does not work with you. please save your money and time, take someone else!

[Joseph Elliot · CS251 · Q4.0/D3.0 · Newark]
Personally he's not the best nor is he the worst, but he is quite nice whenever it comes to questions. If you are someone who is independent and a self study person, then take him.

[Joseph Elliot · CS102 · Q3.0/D3.0 · Newark]
professor elliot is okay, he does have an accent but it is easy to understand. His lectures consist of him reading from the power-point slides. attendance is mandatory so try to arrive to class. his labs are 3% which is an easy 100 even if don't get it right. however, he does not know how to help students when it comes to the midterm or final exam.

[Joseph Elliot · CS347 · Q1.0/D4.0 · Newark]
This professor does not teach at all. He gave everyone the source code for the projects in the book and just told us to make it work for the projects. No help AT ALL when we ran into bugs, just tells you to try again. The grades are just based on the book projects, if you can't get them to run, good luck passing. Strongly suggest avoiding this guy.

[Joseph Elliot · CS335 · Q4.0/D3.0 · Newark]
Voice is monotone. Project based. Will take again.

title: Jerry Illanovsky

units.text: The worst.. He freestyles HW prompts on the spot, only to forget key instructions that result in hours and hours of debugging. Gets angry when you ask for help. Someone asked him to zoom in on the projector because the text was so small, and he began smashing the keyboard until only half a word took up the whole screen. Yikes.. avoid avoid avoid

Other students in the thread have mentioned this but there was a situation where he harassed a student by yelling in his face. Other than that, the main issue is his teaching ability. He has absolutely no idea how to teach. If you are actually trying to learn CS this teacher isn't for you. Also your grade is completely based on his mood.

Awesome professor I would take him again. There were 5 exams including midterm and final but he only gave so many so that there wasn't too much material on each exam. He is lenient and would tell you what to expect on exams so that it wasn't too difficult. Homeworks were easy but sometimes lengthy. Attendance mandatory but not participation. 10/10.

Taking this professor has not only been useless, but very boring. What is done in class and for homework does not whatsoever aid your understanding of the material. For our exams we were expected to know the material like that back of our hands, creating program that was reviewed for barely half of a class, and database that was talked about once.

Prof. Illanovsky is by far the worst prof. I have ever met. There is 0 structure to his class. Your grade is up to him, whether he wants to give you an A, B, C, F, he chooses at random. I can promise taking his class, will not only confuse you, but it will make you reconsider being a computer science student. I can promise it will be a bad time.

Multiple Exams with unreasonable expectations. Had 1hr 20m for a two part exam (50pts each.) 3 page written portion and then a c-program. Most of the class ran out of time and lost 50 pts. He was not flexible. The next test, he did the same thing. Same amount of time and weight. Program and database in the given time. 2/3 of class didn't get 50/100

Good luck learning. All the assignments and homework are basically given to you to just submit. I have applied to many internship positions and with the assessments consisting of data structures, I was furious that I took his class. Nothing is ever graded, and he just talks in class. I got a good grade, but learned absolutely NOTHING!

BEST CS PROFESSOR EVER!! Everyone fights to join his class, he is always the first to fill up during registration. Funny and caring, very sweet. Class is super easy. Easy assignments, easy exams, you will get an A

Jerry tries to help everyone pass. He often gets sidetracked but tries to make python easy to understand. If you already know very basic python this is light. Try to talk to him, participate, or make jokes and he'll definitely help you at the end of the semester if you need it, which you won't unless you just don't turn in anything. I recommend him
Great professor, will to take it again! Very Informational, his teaching method goes beyond boring lectures. He uses real world examples to explain new materials.

I took Professor Jerry for both Computer and Programming I and II, he is the best. He cares about his students understanding the programs and is very fair with homework. I rented the book from Chegg and went over all his power point slides and got an A. As long as you do all the homework on time, review what's been taught in class, you will pass.

Overall a good professor, tries to keep class interesting can really tell he does care about you learning and the students. I would take again. Don't miss class its not the end of the world but it is helpful to be in class.

Best CS professor!! Really kind and easy-going. I had him for CS101 as well, everyone fights to get him on registration day. If you had past experience with coding, this should be easy for you. Exams are super easy, he sends a simple study guide with answers in them. 10/10

Best professor to take for intensive programming. Yes, it is very time consuming and a lot of hw but they are pretty doable as long as you put in the time and effort to study hard. Take him if you get a chance.

one of the easiest professors at rutgers. lectures were based off of slides but prof. jerry did his best with explaining every single thing. homework, in-class assignments, and two midterm exams with one final exam are very easy. no quizzes. he is amazing. take him if you can, he makes computer science very easy for beginners!

Best professor I ever had in CS. A lot of programming work in C,PHP, MYSQL. His approach is casual and friendly. He cares about his students, and most of the time he accesible after class for as long as needed. The course was time consuming, and hard, but his approach made it feel as a challenge he was in, with us. Take him you won't regret it
He is AMAZINGGG!!! Definitely take him, no doubt. The tests are hard but he is very understanding and is willing to curve.

title: Bruno Richard

units.text: Took his Data Viz class this past semester and I highly recommend it. Not an easy class by any means but he is very fair and you will learn A LOT. Lectures are vital. If you plan on missing more than one lecture do not take this class. He is a challenging professor but it forces you to think and learn which I really enjoyed. Overall great.

Tough class but works with you whenever you need the chance. Gives a lot of feedback and is always accessible during his office hours. Class is very project heavy and no exams. Assignments took weeks to do. But like i said he will work with you and the course is in R and you use atom.

i took this course as an elective for computer science and it was honestly very hard. we had four homework assignments which took two weeks to complete. i tried my best and still couldn't get the grade i wanted. no exams except for the final project. mostly code in R and atom. professor bruno is very nice tho, he tries to keep the class engaged.

clean_text:

Took his Data Viz class this past semester and I highly recommend it. Not an easy class by any means but he is very fair and you will learn A LOT. Lectures are vital. If you plan on missing more than one lecture do not take this class. He is a challenging professor but it forces you to think and learn which I really enjoyed. Overall great.

title: Nicole Richardson

units.text: one of the easiest professors ever -- gives a lot of extra credit, the course is incredibly easy (you could learn the entire curriculum in under a week), and is reasonable with grading.

She's a nice prof, a little intimidating at times, but she's not that bad like other people say she is. She's just looking for specific answers at times, and she usually tries getting answers before telling you it. She allows us to be a TA for some classes so we can get a hands-on experience. Overall, not the worst prof at rutgers, just passionate.

This professor could be better if she didn't just give lectures all the time and made us do actual hands on work. We barely coded in class and it's hard to pay attention when it's just pure lecture. Students won't learn unless we DO the work, not watch how someone else does it. It's unfortunate that she's the only person to teach this class.
professor richardson is strict, but not the kind of strict where you get discouraged. instead she inspires you to be better. i would definitely take a class from her again.

While this professor clearly has extensive industry knowledge, their arrogance overshadows any value they could bring to the classroom. They are the epitome of how knowledge without wisdom leads to condescending, ineffective teaching that can leave students frustrated and uninspired.

Prof Richardson is a brilliant person, she knows how to talk, but her lectures need some work. She is very critical of work, although in a field like this I see why that seems necessary. She is almost impossible to access outside of class. The class seems a bit incoherent and the group project feels very sudden with little time to complete.

Hands down one of the best professors to take if you want great skills as a data analyst/scientist. She gives exposure to real-world projects and internships! Very straightforward corporate-style feedback; if you are serious you will quickly realize she could easily be the head of some Fortune 500 institution and choose to teach instead.

I really do hate these softies rating this professor. Even though she does need some improvement on delivering feedback, she is an amazing person to learn from. I believe she is doing students a great favor by teaching very useful material that can be applied to every field. I suggest you taking her if you want to learn. She is not an easy A.
As an educator myself, I must say that this is an awful professor. Very narcissistic and entitled and it is so unfortunate because as of now, she is the only teacher teaching this class.

Her classes are not "Easy As", she is a tough grader but gives you feedback on your work. You get what you put into this class, basically. She is accessible outside of class and I recommend reaching out for extra help - but I did learn a lot of useful concepts in EDD. Overall her classes are tough but she really cares and wants everyone to learn

Professor Richardson is a very challenging professor, but she's very intentional with everything she does, for a good reason. Don't expect the class to be an easy A, but you'll learn a LOT in Everyday Data, and I highly recommend taking it. She also has a ton of experience as a Data Scientist, so you'll earn lots of valuable insight from her.

As of now the only professor for this class. She just makes you copy a bunch of code for every class and expects you to know it. Does not bother teaching any parameters and often struggles with code herself. Isn't helpful when you ask questions. Very tough grader. Calls on everyone. Literally the personification of a trick question. Never again...

She'll be really hard on you, not teach and largely just not recommend her as a teacher, otherwise you'll find yourself getting screwed by them, as they won't teach you a single thing yet expect you to just know most of it all at once.
She was a good professor, however, she was the hardest grader out there. She was super hard when it came to python and data science.
